message:
chore(beads): update tracker with preloaded RunMeta and runtime-mode contract progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-8k83","title":"fix: potential u16 overflow in Painter::for_area multiplication","description":"In canvas.rs Painter::for_area(), the calculation 'area.width * mode.cols_per_cell()' and 'area.height * mode.rows_per_cell()' performs u16 * u16 which can overflow. For example, if area.width=40000 and mode=Braille (cols=2), 40000*2=80000 overflows u16. Should use saturating_mul to prevent silent overflow.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:09:25.948267964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:11:16.326605678Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T19:11:16.326394070Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Changed Painter::for_area() to use saturating_mul for width/height calculation to prevent u16*u16 overflow. All 24 canvas tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Optimize or adjust algorithm to meet word movement budget.","acceptance_criteria":"1) editor_perf word-move budget passes at target sizes.\n2) Structured perf diagnostics (p50/p95/p99, sample count, variance) emitted.\n3) No behavior change; editor correctness tests still pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:15:40.429960338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:45.835790177Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:45.835772454Z","close_reason":"Optimized move_word_right (no alloc) + test passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-8vstx","title":"task(runtime-perf): define user-visible degraded-mode, responsiveness, and recovery semantics","description":"Background:
The current runtime lane emphasizes queueing, admission control, and non-interference, but it does not yet encode a crisp contract for what users and operators should observe when the system is under pressure. Optimization should improve perceived responsiveness and clarity, not merely reduce internal cost.

Goal:
Define the user-visible degraded-mode, responsiveness, and recovery contract that runtime optimization work must satisfy.","design":"Work packages:
- Define the user-visible semantics of healthy, stressed, degraded, and recovered runtime modes using measured runtime instrumentation as the grounding evidence rather than waiting for a final scheduler policy.
- Specify what signals should be exposed through logs, doctor artifacts, or UI-facing hooks when degradation or fallback is active.
- Record recovery expectations such as how the system returns to normal mode and how that transition is explained.
- Identify which user-facing behaviors must remain strict even under load and which can degrade with explicit policy.
- Define scenario classes for normal-path, challenge-path, and negative-control user journeys so later runtime benchmarks, comparators, and gauntlets can test lived responsiveness and recovery instead of only internal scheduler metrics.
- Make this bead an upstream contract for later queue/admission, governor, comparator, non-interference, and rollout work so user experience shapes the policy instead of being inferred after the fact.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Future runtime work has a self-contained contract for what users and operators should experience under load and after recovery.
2. Degraded-mode behavior becomes explicit, testable, and reviewable rather than an emergent side effect of scheduler changes.
3. The bead makes it easier to judge whether an optimization actually improved the lived user experience.
4. The contract is incomplete unless it defines observable signals, scenario classes, and bounded graceful-fallback expectations that later unit/property and scripted E2E validations can check directly.","notes":"This bead now intentionally sits earlier in the runtime lane. The planning correction is that degraded-mode semantics should constrain later control-policy design and implementation, not emerge from them accidentally. This is also the anchor that keeps runtime performance work tied to what users actually feel under load, recovery, and challenge conditions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:03:41.519695525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T20:48:07.149841021Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","ftui-runtime","load-shedding","performance","runtime","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8vstx","depends_on_id":"bd-4flji","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:18.530874806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-945xs","title":"task(pane-core): invariant checker, validator diagnostics, and auto-repair strategy","description":"Implement a validator that can inspect any pane tree and report invariant violations with actionable diagnostics. Add optional repair mode for recoverable issues (e.g., ratio normalization, orphan cleanup).\n\nDeliverables\n- Validation report schema.\n- Repair pass with deterministic behavior and safety guardrails.\n- Logging hooks for diagnostics.\n\nAcceptance\n- Corrupted states are detected reliably.\n- Repair mode never fabricates ambiguous topology; unsafe cases are rejected.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Implement scoped pane-core behavior with deterministic tie-break rules.\n2) Add focused unit tests for normal, boundary, and invalid mutation scenarios.\n3) Add/extend property or fuzz checks where algorithmic invariants are affected.\n4) Ensure failure logs include operation IDs, relevant node IDs, and before/after state hashes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:31:52.556079142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:33:42.883744179Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T07:33:42.883725394Z","close_reason":"Completed: structured invariant report + deterministic safe repair pass (ratio normalization, parent repair, orphan cleanup) with diagnostics and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","invariants","pane","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-945xs","depends_on_id":"bd-1qkzq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-945xs","depends_on_id":"bd-23pre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-950w","title":"Semantic Events: Comprehensive Test Suite","description":"## Unit Tests (60+)

### Click Detection (12 tests)
- Single click detection
- Double click within threshold
- Double click timeout expiry
- Triple click detection
- Click position tolerance
- Different button handling
- Click after drag cancels multi-click
- Rapid clicking beyond triple

### Drag Detection (15 tests)
- Drag threshold respected
- DragStart position correct
- DragMove delta calculation
- DragEnd positions correct
- DragCancel on Escape
- Drag with different buttons
- Drag tolerance for small movements
- Drag state reset on mouse up
- No drag if threshold not met
- Drag across screen boundaries

### Long Press (8 tests)
- Long press triggers after threshold
- Movement cancels long press
- Mouse up cancels long press
- Long press position correct
- Long press duration correct
- Multiple long press in sequence
- Long press with modifier keys

### Chords (10 tests)
- Two-key chord detection
- Three-key chord detection
- Chord timeout expiry
- Chord buffer clear on non-chord key
- Modifier-only doesn't start chord
- Overlapping chord sequences
- Case sensitivity

### Swipe (8 tests)
- Horizontal swipe detection
- Vertical swipe detection
- Velocity calculation correct
- Direction determination
- Swipe disabled by default
- Minimum distance threshold
- Swipe vs drag disambiguation

### Integration (7 tests)
- Events in correct order
- State machine reset
- Focus loss handling
- Multiple gesture types
- Performance under load

## E2E Script

`scripts/semantic_events_e2e.sh`:
- Simulate input sequences
- Verify event output
- Measure latency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 60+ unit tests
- [ ] E2E script working
- [ ] Coverage > 90%
- [ ] Performance benchmarks

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.

Coverage: click detection (12 tests including position tolerance, multi-button, boundary positions), drag detection (12 tests including threshold, delta, vertical, cancel), long press (6 tests), chord detection (7 tests), config (5 tests), integration/edge cases (15 tests). All 480 ftui-core tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:49:44Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mevj","title":"Implement Headless Rendering Mode tests","description":"# Implement Headless Rendering Mode tests

## Background
Part of Headless Rendering Mode (bd-3m7u). Tests ensure headless mode works for CI testing.

## Unit Tests
- headless_term_processes_ansi_correctly: CSI sequences update screen state
- cursor_movement_works: CSI H, CSI A/B/C/D move cursor correctly
- style_state_tracked: SGR sequences update style
- screen_text_returns_content: screen_text() matches expected output
- assert_matches_works: Assertion helper functions correctly

## Integration Tests
- snapshot_test_workflow: Full snapshot test with HeadlessTerm
- complex_layout_renders_correctly: Nested widgets render as expected
- style_codes_produce_correct_output: Colors and attributes work

## Property Tests
- any_ansi_sequence_processed_safely: Malformed sequences dont crash
- cursor_stays_in_bounds: Cursor never goes outside screen

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests verify real CI workflow
- [ ] Headless mode matches real terminal behavior
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:13.872368352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:45:40.978575591Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:45:40.978505329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mevj","depends_on_id":"bd-3m7u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-mn4a0","title":"task(doctor-perf): eliminate duplicate subprocess and comparison work in high-cost doctor paths","description":"Background:\nRecent review work already showed how easy it is for capture/observe paths to duplicate app launches or split observation from the intended target. More broadly, doctor workflows can burn large amounts of time by redundantly launching, comparing, or re-deriving information that should be shared.\n\nGoal:\nImplement the first doctor optimization lever by removing provably redundant workflow work while preserving artifact quality and replayability.","design":"Work packages:\n- Use the workflow cost map to identify the highest-value redundant subprocess or comparison patterns.\n- Keep the implementation centered on one primary redundancy-elimination lever at a time.\n- Require explicit reasoning about how the change preserves captured evidence, summary correctness, and replay commands.\n- Define conservative fallback behavior when the deduplication conditions are not met.\n- Record what before/after artifacts should prove the savings and the preserved semantics.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead has a tight scope centered on removing redundant work, not broadly making doctor faster.
2. Evidence quality and replayability are explicit review criteria.
3. The task text gives future reviewers enough context to judge whether a proposed implementation stayed aligned with project goals.
4. The implementation is incomplete without focused unit/integration tests plus scripted failure-path E2E evidence and detailed logs proving when deduplication did or did not apply.","notes":"This bead should favor clarity over cleverness. A slightly smaller win that preserves operator trust is better than an aggressive dedupe strategy that makes failures harder to understand.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:34.449422623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:05:05.795780391Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","diagnostics","doctor_frankentui","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-dyr5a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:30.627460470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:27.614924008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-qbbkv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:24.626317771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

A slightly smaller win that preserves operator trust is better than an aggressive dedupe strategy that makes failures harder to understand.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:34.449422623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T02:44:42.755923604Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T02:44:42.755611149Z","close_reason":"Completed: deduplicated suite/report run_meta loading, added proof logs, and expanded tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","diagnostics","doctor_frankentui","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-dyr5a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:30.627460470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:27.614924008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mn4a0","depends_on_id":"bd-qbbkv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:24.626317771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-muv6p","title":"task(perf-foundation): build deterministic macrobenchmark and replay fixture suites","description":"Background:\nThe program needs repeatable workloads that are representative of real use rather than tiny microbenchmarks. These fixtures should exercise render-heavy screens, runtime stress paths, and doctor workflows in ways that can be replayed consistently and compared across revisions.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the canonical fixture suites and replay assets used by baseline capture, profiling, parity checks, and tail-latency gauntlets.","design":"Work packages:
- Translate the inventory bead into concrete fixture families for render, runtime, and doctor lanes.
- Partition fixtures into canonical regression suites and adversarial/challenge suites so the project gets both deterministic repeatability and robustness coverage.
- Ensure fixtures capture representative state transitions such as sparse updates, large invalidations, resize churn, subscription churn, cancellations, timeouts, degraded I/O, and artifact-heavy diagnostic runs.
- Define reproducibility rules: seeds, timing controls, host fingerprints, and fixture versioning.
- Specify artifact naming so baseline/profile/replay/challenge data can be compared over time.
- Include explicit notes on why each fixture exists, which user/operator pain it approximates, and whether it is expected to demonstrate wins, graceful fallback, or negative-control stability.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The project has deterministic fixtures that future performance work can reuse without reinventing workloads.
2. Each fixture is traceable to an explicit optimization question or invariant from the inventory bead.
3. Fixture metadata is rich enough to support profiling, shadow comparison, and later rollout scorecards.
4. The suite explicitly includes adversarial challenge fixtures and negative-control cases so optimizations cannot quietly overfit to a narrow curated benchmark set.","notes":"Planning correction: the fixture layer now explicitly distinguishes canonical regression fixtures from challenge fixtures. That protects users from benchmark-specialized optimizations that would otherwise look excellent in curated runs but regress rarer real-world regimes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:24.344944735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:13:12.218077994Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:13:12.217570453Z","close_reason":"Implemented fixture_suite.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","benchmarking","extreme-software-optimization","fixtures","ftui-performance-control-plane","observability","performance","performance-foundation"]}
 {"id":"bd-n29iy","title":"Fix inline overlay log truncation to respect display width","description":"InlineRenderer::sanitize_overlay_log_line truncates by char count instead of display columns. Wide glyphs (e.g. CJK) can still overflow term width and wrap into the UI region, violating overlay safety. Use unicode-width column accounting and add regressions for wide-char clamp/exact-fit behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"WildMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:13:35.308808074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:00:32.035730950Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:00:32.035694202Z","close_reason":"Already fixed: InlineRenderer::sanitize_overlay_log_line uses UnicodeWidthChar display-width accounting and has wide-char regression tests in ftui-core/src/inline_mode.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-rvz9p","title":"task(galaxy-brain): Implement live posterior sparkline widget","description":"Real-time sparkline showing posterior probability evolution over last N frames for each decision domain. Color-coded: green=confident, yellow=uncertain, red=fallback zone. -{"id":"bd-rw97d","title":"task(perf-rollout): define the unified performance evidence ledger and artifact contract","description":"Background:\nBy the time multiple optimization lanes are active, the project needs one place where baseline IDs, hotspot IDs, proof artifacts, replay commands, gauntlet runs, and rollout verdicts can be joined coherently. Without that, the optimization program will generate evidence faster than humans can understand it.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the shared artifact contract and evidence ledger that all performance lanes feed into and all rollout decisions consume.","design":"Work packages:
- Define the canonical identifiers and file/report schema linking workflow inventory, baselines, profiles, proofs, implementations, gauntlets, rollout decisions, and validation-matrix outputs.
- Reuse and extend the existing artifact-manifest, retention, redaction, and failure-signature planning beads rather than inventing a second artifact vocabulary.
- Explain how render, runtime, and doctor artifacts map into the same ledger without losing lane-specific detail.
- Specify required versus optional fields so missing evidence becomes a visible defect.
- Include explicit schema for detailed logs: stable IDs, reason codes, mismatch classes, artifact references, and replay commands.
- Record how humans and CI should navigate from a failed verdict to the relevant artifacts and replay commands.
- Treat this bead as an early information-architecture contract: it should be definable before every artifact-producing implementation bead is complete, while still being rich enough to absorb their outputs later without churn.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every major artifact produced by the optimization program has a defined place in a unified ledger.
2. Missing or malformed evidence becomes easy to detect and reason about.
3. Future scorecards and rollout drills can rely on this bead directly rather than inventing their own artifact vocabulary.
4. The ledger is rich enough to explain test coverage completeness and detailed failure logging, not just pass/fail outcomes.","notes":"Planning correction: the ledger contract should be defined early enough to shape artifact-producing work, not only after all baselines and hotspots already exist. That makes later lane outputs more consistent and reduces schema drift. Additional refinement: artifact completeness is not enough; the ledger must also minimize diagnosis latency for humans and CI.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:36.307538392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T01:40:45.908979387Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","artifacts","ci","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance","performance-rollout","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-17ar5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:09:13.341300536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-2tg0i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:08:52.875462174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-9v2se","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:14:49.417674613Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:54.678020321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-pou7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:09.496835497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-xl1c4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:09:25.949626063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-sfrv","title":"Runtime event parity + inline correctness hardening","description":"Harden the runtime and event pipeline so inline mode behaves predictably and event semantics are consistent across input paths.

Scope:
- Tick scheduling must deliver deterministic model updates with explicit semantics and no busy-spin.
- Inline mode must use UI-height frame sizing and consistent buffers.
- Cursor and scroll-region behavior must prevent log writes from overwriting UI.
- Input parser vs runtime event path parity for kitty keyboard, BackTab or Null or Backspace, mouse drag or scroll, and modifiers.

Deliverables:
- Precise runtime semantics documented in code and tests.
- Comprehensive unit tests and E2E scripts (PTY capture, JSON summary, reproduction commands, durations).
- No regressions in alt-screen or existing harness behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- All dependent tasks complete and tests pass.
- E2E logs include enough detail to debug failures quickly.
- Runtime behavior is deterministic and documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:28:14.983185628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:49:20.079177401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:49:20.079153837Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sfrv","depends_on_id":"bd-1nz8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sfrv","depends_on_id":"bd-20vg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sfrv","depends_on_id":"bd-3ufh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sfrv","depends_on_id":"bd-xh8s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
